methods.name: Peer-Median

data.narrative:
Picogrid is a Series A-stage company in Defense & Security that has raised $45M+. The valuation analysis relies on a comparable universe of 10 companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private peers. The peer set for quantitative triangulation comprises 4 same-stage, same-sector companies: Stark Defence ($500M), Vatn Systems ($300M), Ares Industries ($150M), and NODA AI ($125M).

Two methods were applied. Stage-progression, using total raised ($45M) as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a valuation of $90M. Peer-median triangulation across the four identified comparables yields a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $125M to $500M. These methods anchor the analysis at materially different levels, reflecting both Picogrid's funding stage and the heterogeneous valuations present in the Defense & Security frontier-tech cohort.

Triangulating across both methods yields a range of $76M (low) to $259M (high), with a median of $158M. This wide band reflects genuine uncertainty around where Picogrid sits relative to its peer set on technology maturity, customer traction, and defensibility. The peer-median approach likely better reflects current Defense & Security market pricing, while stage-progression provides a conservative floor. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, regulatory barriers, and competitive positioning within the peer cohort.
